name: Run
description: Default faster ground locomotion that converts directional input into responsive travel while preserving collision, animation, and camera readability.
design_notes: Run usually becomes the main locomotion state, so it must support repeated use without hiding important environment reads. Decide how quickly characters reach full pace, how they stop, how they rotate, and how slopes or surface materials alter the movement response. It should be fast enough for traversal but restrained enough that sprint, dash, mounts, and traversal tools still matter.
design_purpose: Make ordinary navigation efficient across combat arenas, hubs, and exploration spaces.
Provide a default locomotion state that remains readable for animation, camera, and collision.
Create a stable base for modifiers such as sprint, crouch, stamina drain, or aiming movement.
player_fantasy: The player feels capable and responsive, moving through the world with confidence rather than waiting on the avatar.

parameters.name: run_pace_units_per_second
parameters.description: Target horizontal velocity for the default run state.

parameters.name: run_acceleration_curve_id
parameters.description: Curve used when entering run from idle or walk.

parameters.name: run_deceleration_curve_id
parameters.description: Curve used when input_vector drops or reverses.

parameters.name: run_turn_degrees_per_second
parameters.description: Facing rotation budget while running under player control.

parameters.name: run_slope_limit_degrees
parameters.description: Ground incline accepted before run changes to slide, climb, or blocked movement.

parameters.name: run_prediction_window_ms
parameters.description: Client-side movement window reconciled against server_tick in networked games.

edge_cases: Run should not preserve full velocity when stepping from ground into a ladder, ledge grab, or scripted traversal state.
Sharp input reversal near thin collision can create oscillation if deceleration and collision projection fight each other.
Camera-relative input must update correctly when lock-on or cinematic camera changes orientation mid-run.
Server correction can snap a running player backward if client_input_sequence ordering is not deterministic.
common_bugs: Run pace is applied directly to transform position and bypasses collision sweeps on fast frames.
Animation blend trees use raw stick magnitude and never match resolved ground velocity on slopes.
Surface material modifiers stack permanently after crossing mud, ice, or moving platforms.
Input buffering from dodge, slide, or sprint leaves run state active after stun or menu focus.
balancing_notes: Run pace sets the expected density of levels; increasing it may require wider lanes and earlier enemy telegraphs.
Strong acceleration feels responsive but reduces the difference between run, dash, and dodge roll.
Networked action games often benefit from slightly lower maximum run pace to reduce correction severity.
accessibility_notes: Offer always-run or toggle-run options when holding a modifier would be fatiguing.
Keep camera sway, field-of-view shifts, and head bob optional or adjustable during sustained running.

implementation_notes.unity: Resolve run state before movement integration, then apply acceleration curves inside FixedUpdate. Use CharacterController.Move or Rigidbody motion with collision sweeps, keep Animator parameters tied to post-collision velocity, and include server_tick plus client_input_sequence for network reconciliation.
implementation_notes.godot: Use CharacterBody velocity as the source of truth. Apply run acceleration in _physics_process, pass floor normals into slope checks, and update AnimationTree after move_and_slide returns collision information.
implementation_notes.unreal: Configure CharacterMovement for running but keep sprint, aim, root motion, and traversal overrides as explicit state transitions. In replicated play, let the server validate movement mode changes and reconcile predicted run inputs.
implementation_notes.web: Update run in a fixed-step controller using input_vector, acceleration_curve_id, and collision layer masks. Render interpolation should never feed back into simulation position, especially when prediction_window_ms is active.

example_games.title: Super Mario Odyssey
example_games.note: Uses responsive running as a baseline for jumps, captures, and traversal.

example_games.title: Hollow Knight
example_games.note: Uses a consistent run pace as the base for combat and platforming movement.
